name: Shakuntala
description: Shakuntala is a prominent figure in Hindu mythology, best known as the mother of Emperor Bharata and a central character in Kalidasa's celebrated play, *Abhijnanashakuntalam*. Her story begins in the forest, where she is raised by the sage Kanva after being abandoned by her celestial parents, Menaka, an apsara, and Vishwamitra, a powerful sage. King Dushyanta encounters Shakuntala during a hunting trip and falls deeply in love with her. They marry in a Gandharva Vivaha, a marriage based on mutual consent. Dushyanta is then compelled to return to his kingdom, promising to send for her soon and leaving her with a royal ring as a token of his love and recognition. While Shakuntala waits, she absentmindedly disrespects the sage Durvasa, who curses her so that Dushyanta will forget her until he sees the ring. Due to unfortunate circumstances, the ring is lost before she can meet Dushyanta again, leading to a period of hardship and rejection. Eventually, the ring is recovered, Dushyanta regains his memory, and Shakuntala is reunited with him. She is revered for her beauty, grace, patience, and her role as the mother of a great emperor, symbolizing resilience and the importance of dharma. Her story explores themes of love, loss, memory, and the consequences of actions, highlighting the importance of recognizing and upholding one's responsibilities.
subtitle: Mother of Emperor Bharata, Forest Nymph

keyMyths.title: Birth and Abandonment
keyMyths.description: Shakuntala was born to the apsara Menaka and the sage Vishwamitra. Menaka abandoned her in the forest to return to Indra's court, where she was found and raised by Sage Kanva.

keyMyths.title: The Curse of Durvasa
keyMyths.description: While lost in thoughts of Dushyanta, Shakuntala failed to properly greet the sage Durvasa. Angered, Durvasa cursed her that Dushyanta would forget her until he saw a token of remembrance.

keyMyths.title: Reunion and Recognition
keyMyths.description: After the recovery of the ring, Dushyanta remembered Shakuntala and their marriage. He sought her out and brought her to his kingdom, recognizing Bharata as his son.

sources.author: Vyasa
sources.description: The epic where Shakuntala's story is first found.
sources.text: The epic where Shakuntala's story is first found.

sources.author: Kalidasa
sources.description: The most famous dramatization of Shakuntala's story.
